section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: Cron jobs crash silently — missed runs are never recovered
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [REDDIT-BURN] r/openclaw founder thread; [ISSUE-10164] 13 upvotes, no maintainer response since Feb 2026
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Temporal Schedule replaces in-process setTimeout. Schedule lives on Temporal Server, survives Worker death. catchupWindow policy queues missed triggers.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: Catch-up window has a configurable limit (default 10 min). Triggers missed beyond the window are dropped by policy — this is intentional, not a gap.

section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: No execution history or audit trail — no way to see what happened after a crash
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [ISSUE-10164] 'No unified view of running/pending work'; Temporal's native UI provides this
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Temporal Event History records every Activity completion. Temporal UI shows running/pending/failed Workflows. Every action is on the timeline.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: Temporal UI requires Temporal Server deployment. Local dev server provides this out of the box.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.crash_matrix_ref: All CP entries — Event History is the recovery authority

section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: Retries duplicate side effects — same Slack message sent twice, same file written twice
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [VERCEL-7261] 3-5% duplicate tool call rate with different tool call IDs; [PYDANTIC-83] read/write distinction proposed, closed as docs question
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Completed Activities return cached results on replay. Idempotency keys prevent duplicate writes. SER router classifies reads vs writes and applies different retry policies.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: External APIs without native idempotency support require application-level dedup keys. Tenure generates these but the external service must honor them.

section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: Gateway restart drops all session state — in-flight work is lost
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [ISSUE-62442] Apr 7 2026, confirms session state is in-memory, not persisted. Official docs: 'Events are not replayed; clients must refresh on gaps.'
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Tenure Workflows do not depend on Gateway for execution coordination. Temporal Server is the coordination authority. Gateway becomes a UI/notification layer.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: Gateway restart still affects OpenClaw's own UI/notification delivery. Tenure only protects execution, not the chat experience.

section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: Conversation history saves to disk but doesn't reload correctly after crash
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [ISSUE-55343] 'History saves to disk but doesn't load — data exists, recovery broken'
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Temporal Event History is the recovery authority, not .jsonl transcripts. Replay reconstructs execution state from Activity completions. OpenClaw's transcript is a secondary log.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: OpenClaw's conversation display may still show gaps if .jsonl is corrupted. Tenure protects execution recovery, not conversation display.

section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: Session memory and summaries not flushed before reset — critical context lost at the worst time
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [ISSUE-21382] 'Session memory markdown not flushed before reset'
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Tool results are recorded as Activity completions in Temporal history BEFORE delivery to the next reasoning step. The 30-second best-effort flush is no longer the persistence path.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: Compaction summaries are still non-deterministic (blocker B-2). Tenure treats compaction as a side-effecting Activity to cache the summary.

section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_complaint: No budget cap or runaway protection — fear of max token burn
section_a_seven_gap_closure.community_source: [REDDIT-BURN] 'Tired of cron jobs crashes and fear of max token burn'
section_a_seven_gap_closure.tenure_mechanism: Budget enforcement in Workflow logic, evaluated before each Activity dispatch. Deterministic on replay — survives Worker death. Circuit breaker trips on pathological loops.
section_a_seven_gap_closure.remaining_risk: Budget tracking accuracy depends on cost data from the LLM provider. If provider cost reporting is delayed, the cap may be hit slightly late.

section_b_framework_failure_modes.failure_mode: No durable state — agent execution lives entirely in process memory. Crash loses all progress.
section_b_framework_failure_modes.tenure_test_case: Run a multi-step LangChain agent through Tenure. SIGKILL mid-execution. Verify replay resumes from last Activity.

section_b_framework_failure_modes.failure_mode: RFC #6617 proposed five production reliability primitives (durable execution, retries, checkpointing, timeouts, observability). None shipped as of April 2026.
section_b_framework_failure_modes.tenure_test_case: All five primitives are covered: Temporal Activities (durable), retry policies (retries), Event History (checkpointing), Activity timeouts (timeouts), Temporal UI (observability).

section_b_framework_failure_modes.failure_mode: No crash recovery mechanism. Multi-agent orchestration is in-process only. Parent death orphans children.
section_b_framework_failure_modes.tenure_test_case: Run a multi-agent workflow with parent-child Workflows. SIGKILL parent. Verify child continues or is cleaned up per ParentClosePolicy.

section_b_framework_failure_modes.failure_mode: Conversation state is in-memory. No execution typing — all tool calls treated identically. No built-in retry differentiation.
section_b_framework_failure_modes.tenure_test_case: Classify tool calls through SER router. Verify reads get cached retry, writes get idempotency keys, critical transactions get HITL gates.

section_b_framework_failure_modes.failure_mode: No execution typing or semantic classification. Agent tools are opaque function calls with no retry differentiation.
section_b_framework_failure_modes.tenure_test_case: Same as AutoGen — SER router provides the classification layer that ADK lacks.

section_b_framework_failure_modes.failure_mode: Issue #83 proposed read/write tool distinction. Samuel Colvin closed it as a documentation question. No enforcement layer.
section_b_framework_failure_modes.tenure_test_case: SER router enforces the read/write distinction that Pydantic AI acknowledged but chose not to implement. Taxonomy provides defaults. execution: block allows authors to declare.

section_c_quantitative_claims.claim: 800,000+ agent skills exist across the ecosystem
section_c_quantitative_claims.reliability: medium — self-reported by a marketplace aggregator, likely includes duplicates and inactive skills
section_c_quantitative_claims.how_tenure_changes_this: Tenure does not change the count. It provides a classification layer (taxonomy + execution: block) so that any of these 800K skills can declare their execution contract.

section_c_quantitative_claims.reliability: high — peer-reviewed academic paper with methodology
section_c_quantitative_claims.how_tenure_changes_this: Tenure's SER router provides an enforcement boundary. Vulnerable skills still exist, but their execution is typed, counted, and revocable. Critical transactions require HITL approval.

section_c_quantitative_claims.claim: 190 security advisories across 10 OpenClaw architectural layers
section_c_quantitative_claims.reliability: high — systematic taxonomy from Texas A&M research group
section_c_quantitative_claims.how_tenure_changes_this: Tenure addresses the execution layer specifically. It does not fix all 190 advisories but it makes the execution boundary auditable and the side effects revocable.

section_c_quantitative_claims.claim: 3-5% duplicate tool call rate in Vercel AI SDK
section_c_quantitative_claims.reliability: high — reported by users with reproduction steps, different tool call IDs on duplicates
section_c_quantitative_claims.how_tenure_changes_this: Temporal's deterministic replay eliminates this class of bug entirely. Completed Activities return cached results. The dedup is structural, not a fragile in-memory cache.

section_c_quantitative_claims.claim: Issue #10164 has 13 upvotes and no maintainer response
section_c_quantitative_claims.how_tenure_changes_this: Tenure is the answer to this issue. External adapter, MIT licensed, proves the guarantee on user's own infrastructure.

section_c_quantitative_claims.claim: LangGraph RFC #6617 proposed five reliability primitives, none shipped
section_c_quantitative_claims.reliability: high — RFC exists, status is observable
section_c_quantitative_claims.how_tenure_changes_this: Tenure ships all five through Temporal: durable execution (Activities), retries (retry policies), checkpointing (Event History), timeouts (Activity timeouts), observability (Temporal UI + OTEL).
